title: The gravity assisted energy conversion device
abstract: The principle aim of this device is to generate Electricity by driving a shaft with the required torque and speed by using the Gravitational force acting towards the Earth's core. Gravity is unidirectional force acting towards the earth, which is harnessed to make a body move in a circular motion. To achieve this, an input is given until a specific number of cycles is achieved, beyond which a part of the output can be given as feedback to the device to avoid manual input, thereby making the system self reliable and feasible.
claims: Claims I CLAIM: 1. A gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus, said apparatus comprising: â¢ a shaft; â¢ atleast one disc mounted on the shaft; and â¢ atleast one pair of equal weights in connection with the disc, said weights located opposite to each other on either side of the disc at a pre-determined distance from a centre of the disc such that said weights on being released from an initial angle a causes the disc to rotate to a final angle, said final angle substantially equal to 360Â°-2a. 2. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the initial angle and the final angle of the shaft are calculated from a vertical axis of the shaft. 3. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the pair of equal weights is in connection with the disc by means of a rod like structure, said rod like structure carrying the weights and held by a hole cut through a thickness of the disc. 4. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the pre-determined distance is proximal to circumference of the disc. 5. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the initial angle a lies in a range between 0Â° and 6Â° . 6. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1, further comprising an input power source for providing power to rotate the disc from the final angle to the initial angle. 7. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 6, wherein the input source is selected from a group comprising: â¢ an external power source independent of the gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus; and â¢ an internal source powered from the energy produced by the gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus. 8. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein the disc and the weights are made from iron. 9. The gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 1, wherein a weight of the weights, dimensions of the weights and the pre-determined distance of the weights from the centre of the disc is based on: â¢ thickness of the disc; â¢ diameter of the disc; â¢ material of the disc; and â¢ moment of inertia of the disc. 10. A method for operating a gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus having a shaft having a vertical axis, atleast one disc mounted on the shaft and atleast one pair of equal weights in connection with the disc, said weights located opposite to each other on either side of the disc at a pre-determined distance from a centre of the disc, said method comprising: â¢ releasing the weights from an initial angle a to cause the disc to rotate to a final angle, said final angle substantially equal to 360Â°-2a; and â¢ providing an input power from an input source to rotate the disc from the final angle to the initial angle. 11. The method for operating a gravity driven electrical generating apparatus as claimed in claim 10 wherein the input power is transmitted with a pipe carrying a DC motor which is parallel to the main shaft and is located between the shaft and the weights. 12. The method for operating a gravity driven electrical generating apparatus as claimed in claim 11 wherein the input power is transmitted at centre of the shaft and substantially close to the rotating disc such that external speed and torque requirements are reduced to minimum. 13. The method for operating a gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus as claimed in claim 10 wherein the input source is selected from a group comprising: â¢ an external power source independent of the gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus; and â¢ an internal source powered from the energy produced by the gravity driven electrical energy generating apparatus.
